summary: This is an ultra-short post from 2004 where Shapiro simply links to an external resource about knife sharpening (or avoiding whetstones) with minimal commentary. The post is essentially a link-share with a humorous sign-off telling readers there's nothing more to see. It reflects the early blogging era's casual, link-curation style.
key_points: The entire post is a single external link recommendation with no substantive analysis
Represents the early 2000s blogging convention of short link-posts with brief commentary
The self-aware sign-off ('No extended entry. Go home.') shows Shapiro's characteristic humor
money_quotes: That's it. No extended entry. Go home.
key_insight: A playful micro-post from the early blogging era that amounts to a single link recommendation with no substantive content to analyze.
